title: The Friends Who Didn't Stay

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I lost friends to chronic illness. <break time="350ms"/>Not all of them. <break time="300ms"/>But enough.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>At first they tried. <break time="300ms"/>Check-in texts. <break time="250ms"/>Plans we both knew I'd cancel. <break time="400ms"/>But people can only handle so many cancelled plans. <break time="350ms"/>So many 'not today's. <break time="300ms"/>So many conversations where I couldn't pretend to be okay. <break time="500ms"/>Some faded quietly. <break time="400ms"/>Others had conversations. <break time="350ms"/>The worst was a friend who said: <break time="300ms"/>'You're not fun anymore.' <break time="500ms"/>She didn't mean to be cruel. <break time="400ms"/>She was just being honest. <break time="350ms"/>And she was right. <break time="300ms"/>I wasn't fun anymore. <break time="250ms"/>I was surviving.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>The friendships that survived are different now. <break time="400ms"/>Deeper, maybe. <break time="350ms"/>More honest. <break time="500ms"/>And the new friends - <break time="400ms"/>the ones who met me sick - <break time="350ms"/>they never knew the other version. <break time="300ms"/>They just know me. <break time="500ms"/>Losing people hurts. <break time="400ms"/>But the ones who stay? <break time="350ms"/>They're the real ones.

when_to_share: When someone is grieving lost friendships or feeling isolated due to illness
